events.title: Birth of Astronomer Galileo Galilei
events.summary: The pioneering astronomer and physicist Galileo Galilei was born in Pisa, Italy.
events.details: Galileo's work laid the foundation for modern physics and astronomy. His observations with the telescope revolutionized our understanding of the cosmos and challenged long-held beliefs about the universe.

events.title: The City of St. Louis is Founded
events.summary: French fur traders established a settlement that would become the major US city of St. Louis.
events.details: Pierre Laclède and Auguste Chouteau founded the trading post near the confluence of the Mississippi and Missouri rivers. It quickly grew into a vital hub for American westward expansion.

events.title: Birth of Suffragist Susan B. Anthony
events.summary: Susan B. Anthony, a pivotal leader in the women's suffrage movement, was born.
events.details: Her tireless advocacy and activism were crucial in the fight for women's right to vote in the United States. She became a powerful and enduring symbol of equality and civil rights.

events.title: Women Win Right to Argue Before SCOTUS
events.summary: A law allowed female attorneys to argue cases before the US Supreme Court.
events.details: President Rutherford B. Hayes signed the 'Act to Relieve Certain Legal Disabilities of Women.' This landmark legislation was a major victory for gender equality in the legal profession.

events.title: The First 'Teddy Bear' is Introduced
events.summary: The first stuffed toy bear, named after President Teddy Roosevelt, went on sale.
events.details: Inspired by a cartoon of the president sparing a bear cub, a Brooklyn toy maker created the 'Teddy Bear.' The toy became an instant success and an enduring childhood classic.

events.title: FDR Survives Assassination Attempt
events.summary: President-elect Franklin D. Roosevelt survived an assassination attempt in Miami, Florida.
events.details: FDR's survival ensured his steady leadership through the Great Depression and World War II. His presidency would go on to profoundly reshape the nation and the world.

events.title: First General-Purpose Computer Unveiled
events.summary: ENIAC, the first electronic general-purpose computer, was formally dedicated at the University of Pennsylvania.
events.details: This technological marvel, a thousand times faster than previous machines, effectively ushered in the digital age. Its invention paved the way for modern computing and all it enables.

events.title: Disney's Animated Classic Cinderella Premieres
events.summary: Walt Disney's animated film Cinderella was released, enchanting audiences and reviving the studio.
events.details: The film was a massive critical and commercial success that brought joy to millions. Its timeless story, artistry, and music have made it a beloved classic for generations.

events.title: Canada Adopts the Maple Leaf Flag
events.summary: Canada's iconic red and white Maple Leaf flag was officially inaugurated in Ottawa.
events.details: Following a long national debate, the new flag was raised over Parliament Hill for the first time. It became a powerful and unifying symbol of Canadian national identity and pride.

events.title: Britain and Ireland Decimalise Currency
events.summary: The United Kingdom and Ireland officially switched to a decimal currency system.
events.details: Known as 'Decimal Day,' this historic change greatly simplified financial transactions. The move modernized the economies of both nations by replacing the complex pounds, shillings, and pence system.

events.title: Sound Recordings Gain Copyright Protection
events.summary: The US granted federal copyright protection to sound recordings for the first time.
events.details: This law protected artists and record labels from unauthorized duplication and distribution of their music. It was a major step in securing the rights and livelihoods of creative professionals.

events.title: Soviet Union Ends War in Afghanistan
events.summary: The Soviet Union announced that its last troops had officially withdrawn from Afghanistan.
events.details: The withdrawal marked the end of a costly and brutal nine-year war. This event was a significant step toward the conclusion of the Cold War and brought relief to many.

events.title: First Draft of Human Genome Published
events.summary: The first complete draft of the human genome was published in the journal Nature.
events.details: This monumental scientific achievement provided a map of our DNA, opening new frontiers in medicine. It has since accelerated research into treating and preventing thousands of diseases.

events.title: Largest Peace Protest in History Occurs
events.summary: Millions worldwide protested against the impending Iraq War in a massive, coordinated demonstration.
events.details: In over 600 cities, people gathered for what became the largest peace protest in history. The event showcased the powerful collective voice of global citizens advocating for peace.

events.title: YouTube Platform Officially Launched
events.summary: The video-sharing platform YouTube was founded, revolutionizing online media and communication.
events.details: Three former PayPal employees activated the 'youtube.com' domain. The site grew into a global phenomenon, democratizing content creation and changing how people share stories and information.
